title: DNS Resolution, Address Classes, Redirects, Proxies, and SSRF · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in validated connection-time dial policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Allowlist HTTP schemes and ports, reject userinfo and ambiguous host syntax, and prohibit unsupported protocols.
Use net/netip to classify all IPv4 and IPv6 answers including mapped, loopback, private, link-local, multicast, unspecified, and metadata ranges.
Bind admitted addresses to DialContext or a controlled egress proxy and verify each new connection and re-resolution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Egress Defense Depth
steps.instruction:
Order the Egress Defense Depth Authorized site auditing and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Egress Defense Depth.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Egress Defense Depth assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Egress Defense Depth, keep Egress Defense Depth connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 4740 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to block unsafe schemes, netip classes, mixed DNS, rebinding, proxy, metadata, and redirect destinations; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine application checks with proxy rules, firewall egress, metadata denial, network namespace, and production observation.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Egress Defense Depth · fault isolation · Debug Egress Defense Depth, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Egress Defense Depth precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Egress Defense Depth · fault isolation · Debug Egress Defense Depth, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go team handles case 5454 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to block unsafe schemes, netip classes, mixed DNS, rebinding, proxy, metadata, and redirect destinations; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine application checks with proxy rules, firewall egress, metadata denial, network namespace, and production observation.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Egress Defense Depth · fault isolation · Debug Egress Defense Depth, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Correct Go code alone can guarantee every network route.

stimulus.title: Egress Defense Depth failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Correct Go code alone can guarantee every network route.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine application checks with proxy rules, firewall egress, metadata denial, network namespace, and production observation.

options.text: Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the egress defense depth · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For egress defense depth · fault isolation, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Egress Defense Depth.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Egress Defense Depth
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-egress-defense-depth-1ccw85" that uses case 1144 to block unsafe schemes, netip classes, mixed DNS, rebinding, proxy, metadata, and redirect destinations. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Egress Defense Depth.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Egress Defense Depth into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Egress Defense Depth, keep Egress Defense Depth connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Publish the incident record · Debug Egress Defense Depth
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Egress Defense Depth: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A debug Go team handles case 8692 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to block unsafe schemes, netip classes, mixed DNS, rebinding, proxy, metadata, and redirect destinations; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine application checks with proxy rules, firewall egress, metadata denial, network namespace, and production observation.
